description:
## Scope

Add to the `diag` module:

```rust
pub fn report_or_exit(diags: &[Diagnostic], sm: &SourceMap);
```

Behavior:
- Render every diagnostic via the existing renderer (prefer
  `Renderer::emit_all` for batch; fall back to per-diag
  `render_one_to_stderr` if simpler).
- If any diagnostic has `Severity::Error`, `process::exit(1)` after
  rendering.
- Otherwise return normally (warnings still printed).

## Why

Today main.rs has ~18 sites of `render_one_to_stderr(...)` immediately
followed by `process::exit(1)`. The fused render+exit pattern has no
shared helper. fz-0z4.6 (E2) is the first consumer; subsequent
migrations land as fz-0z4.E3 follow-up (out of arc).

## Independent

No upstream dependencies. Can land first or last in the arc; the
consumer ticket (fz-0z4.6) takes the dependency edge.

## Tests

- Unit test: empty diag list → returns normally, no output.
- Unit test: warnings only → renders to provided sink, returns
  normally.
- Unit test: any error → renders, then exits 1. (Test via a wrapper
  that catches exit, or factor the predicate into a testable helper
  and exit at the wrapper level.)

## Acceptance

- `cargo build` clean.
- New unit tests pass.
title: Add diag::report_or_exit shared sink
